ggcheck 0.0.5

Is the ith geom what it should be?

Source: R/geoms.R
ith_geom_is.Rd

ith_geom_is checks whether the ith layer uses the prescribed type of geom.

Usage

ith_geom_is(p, geom, i = 1)

Arguments

p

A ggplot object

geom

A character string that corresponds to the suffix of a ggplot2 geom_ function, e.g. "point".

i

A numerical index that corresponds to the first layer of a plot (1), the second layer (2), and so on. ith_geom_is will check the geom used by the ith layer.

Value

TRUE or FALSE

See also

Other functions for checking geoms: get_geoms(), get_geoms_stats(), ith_geom(), ith_geom_stat(), uses_geoms()

Examples

require(ggplot2)
p <- ggplot(data = mpg, mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) +
  geom_point(mapping = aes(color = class)) +
  geom_smooth()
ith_geom_is(p, geom = "smooth", i = 2)
#> [1] TRUE

On this page

ggcheck is built for use with learnr interactive tutorials.

learnr is a part of the R Markdown ecosystem of packages for creating computational documents in R. Learn more at rmarkdown.rstudio.com.

Developed by Garrick Aden-Buie, Garrett Grolemund, Nischal Shrestha, .
